hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated by Al-Bazanti from Dawud ibn Sirhan, from Abu Abdullah 
{a.s}:  
"The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) 
participated with the Quraysh in the rebuilding of the Kaaba. The portion that came 
under the share of the Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and 
his family) was from the door of the Kaaba to halfway between the Yemeni Corner and 
the Black Stone."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 59 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 216 
• Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 127 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 155 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2323

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
وما إ رإذ إلكعن ه  إ جذ ن سوء إ لأ ع ص ث  إلله عز وج ل لها، ون وى ي وما ي ن غ إلملك  إ ن  ي ق ي ل مق اي له  إ هل إلكعن ه  وي سن ى  ذ ري ي هم ي م 
ي هذم إلكعن ه  ق سالث  عي ن اة جي ى وق عي ا على ج ذي ه  ق سا ل عن  ذ لك ، ق ق الوإ: ما ن زى إلذ ى  إ صان ك  إ لأ ن ما ن وي ث  ق ى  هذ إ إلن ي ث   
لأن  إلن لذ جزم إلله وإلن ي ث  ن ي ث  إلله، و سكان  مكه  ذ ري ه  إ ن زإهي م ج لي ل إلله، ق ق ال: صذق ي م ق ما مح زج ى مما وق عث  ق ي ه؟ ق الوإ: 
ي حذب  ن ق سك  ن ع ي ز ذ لك  ق حذب  ن ق سه ن ح ي ز ق زج عث  جذق ي اة جي ى ي ن ي ي ا ق ى  مكان هما، ق ذعا إلق وم إلذ ي ن  إ س اروإ علي ه ن هذمها 
ق ق ي لهم ي م إ ي ى إلن ي ث  ق كساة إلأن طاع وإ طعم إلطعام ي لاي ي ن  ي وما كل ي وم مان ه  ج ز ور   جي ى جملث  إلح ق ان  إ لى إلسن اع ق ى   
رو وس إلح ن ال ون ي زب  إلأعلاف  للوجوس ، ي م إن صزف  من  مكه  إ لى إلمذي ن ه  ق ا ن ز ل ن ها ق وما من  إ هل إلي من  من  ع سان  وهم  
إلأن صار وروى : إ ن ه ذ ن ح له سي ه  إ لأف  ن ق زة  ن س عث  إن ن  عامز، وكان  ي ق ال لها مطان ح  ي ن غ جي ى ن ز لها إن ن  عامز ق اص ي ق ث  إ لي ه  
ق ق ي ل: س عي ث  إن ن  عامز، ولم ي كن  ي ن غ مو من ا ولأ كاق زإ ولكن ه كان  ممن  ي طلث  إلذي ن  إلحن ي ف ، ولم ي ملك  إلمس زق  إ لأ ي ن غ 
وكسزى. وق صذة إ صحاب  إلق ي ل وملكهم إ ن و ي كسوم: إ ن زهه  ن ن  إلصن اح إلحمي زى  لي هذمه.  ق ا رسل إلله علي هم طي زإ إن ان ي ل 
ي زمي هم ن حح ارة  من  سح ي ل، ق ح علهم كعصف  ما كول.  وإ ن ما لم ي ح ز على إ لحح اح  ما ج زى على ي ن غ وإ صحاب  إلق ي ل لأن  ق صذ  
إلحح اح  لم ي كن  إ لى هذم إلكعن ه  إ ن ما كان  ق صذة إ لى إن ن  إلز ن ي ز وكان  ص ذإ لصاجث  إلحق ، ق لما إسي ح ار ن الكعن ه  إ رإذ إلله إ ن  
ي ن ي ن  للن اس إ ن ه لم ي ح زة ق امهل من  هذمها علي ه.  
2325  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عِي سَّى ن ْن ِ ي ُّون ُّسَّ ق َّالَّ : كَّان َّ إِن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْعَّوْج َّاءِ مِن ْ ي َّلاَّمِذ َّة ِ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلْن َّصْزِى ِّ ق َّان ْح زَّف َّ عَّن ِ 
إَّلي  َّوْجِي ذِ ق َّق ِي لَّ لَّهُّ ي َّزَّكْث َّ مَّذ ْهَّث َّ صَّاجِن ِك َّ وَّ ذَّج َّلْث َّ ق ِي مَّا لأَّ إ َّصْلَّ لَّهُّ وَّ لأَّ جَّق ِي ق َّه َّ ق َّق َّالَّ  إ ِن  َّ صَّاجِن ِى  كَّان َّ مِح ْلَّطاً كَّان َّ 
ي َّق ُّولُّ طَّوْرإً ن ِالْق َّذَّرِ وَّ طَّوْرإً ن ِالْح َّن ْزِ وَّ مَّا إ َّعْلَّمُّهُّ إِعْي َّق َّذَّ مَّذ ْهَّن اً ذَّإمَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ  ق َّالَّ وَّ ذَّج َّلَّ مَّك َّه َّ  ي َّمَّز ُّذإً وَّ إ ِن ْكَّارإً عَّلَّى مَّن ْ 
ي َّحُّح  ُّ وَّ كَّان َّ ي َّكْزَّةُّ إَّلْعُّلَّمَّاءُّ مُّسَّاءَّلَّي َّهُّ إ ِي  َّاهُّمْ وَّ مُّح َّالَّسَّي َّهُّ لَّهُّمْ لِح ُّن ْث ِ لِسَّان ِهِ وَّ ق َّسَّاذِ ص َّمِي زِ ةِ ق َّا َّي َّى ج َّعْق َّزَّ ن ْن َّ مُّحَّم َّذٍ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّح َّلَّسَّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ ق ِى  ج َّمَّاعَّه ٍ مِن ْ ن ُّ ط َّزَّإن ِهِ ي ُّم َّ ق َّالَّ لَّهُّ إ ِن  َّ إَّلْمَّح َّالِسَّ إ َّمَّان َّاب   وَّ لأَّ ن ُّذ َّ لِكُّلِّ مَّن ْ كَّان َّ ن ِهِ سُّعَّال  إ َّن ْ 
ي َّسْعُّلَّ إ َّ ق َّي َّا ْذ َّن ُّ لِى  ق ِى  إَّلْكَّلاَّمِ  ق َّق َّال   ي َّعْن ُّذُّون َّ 
هَّذ َّإ إَّلْن َّي ْث َّ إَّلْمَّزْق ُّوعَّ ن ِالط ُّوب ِ وَّ إَّلْمَّذَّرِ وَّ ي ُّهَّزْوِلُّون َّ جَّوْلَّهُّ هَّزْوَّلَّه َّ إَّلْن َّعِي زِ إ ِذ َّإ ن َّق َّزَّ مَّن ْ ق َّ ك َّزَّ ق ِى  هَّذ َّإ إ َّوْ ق َّذ َّرَّ عَّلِمَّ إ َّن  َّ هَّذ َّإ 
ق ِعْل  إ َّس َّسَّهُّ ع َّي ْزُّ جَّكِي مٍ وَّ لأَّ ذ ِى  ن َّط َّزٍ  ق َّق ُّلْ ق َّا ِن  َّك َّ رَّإ ْسُّ هَّذ َّإ إَّلْأ َّمْزِ وَّ سَّن َّامُّهُّ وَّ إ َّن ُّوك َّ إ ُّس ُّهُّ وَّ ن ِط َّامُّهُّ ق َّق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم   إَّلس  َّي ْطَّان ُّ  وَّلِي  َّهُّ ي ُّورِذُّةُّ  مَّن َّاهِلَّ  
إَّلْهَّلَّكَّه ِ ي ُّم َّ لأَّ ي ُّصْذِرُّةُّ  و هَّذ َّإ ن َّي ْث   إِسْي َّعْن َّذَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ن ِهِ ج َّلْق َّهُّ لِي َّح ْي َّن ِزَّ طَّاعَّي َّهُّمْ ق ِى  إ ِي ْي َّان ِهِ ق َّحَّي  َّهُّمْ عَّلَّى ي َّعْط ِي مِهِ وَّ  ر ِي َّارَّي ِهِ 
وَّ ج َّعَّلَّهُّ مَّحَّل َّ إ َّن ْن ِي َّان ِهِ وَّ ق ِن ْلَّه ً لِلْمُّصَّلِّي ن َّ لَّهُّ ق َّهُّوَّ س ُّعْن َّه   مِن ْ رِص ْوَّإن ِهِ وَّ طَّزِي ق   ي ُّو َّذِّى   إ ِلَّى ع ُّق ْزَّإن ِهِ مَّن ْصُّوب   عَّلَّى إِسْي ِوَّإءِ 
إَّلْكَّمَّالِ وَّ مُّح ْي َّمَّغِ إَّلْعَّط َّمَّه ِ وَّ إَّلْح َّلاَّلِ  ج َّلَّق َّهُّ إَّلل َّهُّ ق َّن ْلَّ ذَّجْوِ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ ن ِا َّلْق َّى ْ عَّامٍ وَّ إ َّجَّق  ُّ مَّن ْ إ ُّطِي غَّ ق ِي مَّا إ َّمَّزَّ وَّ إُّن ْي ُّهِى َّ  عَّم َّا  
ن َّهَّى عَّن ْهُّ وَّ ر َّج َّزَّ إَّلل َّهُّ إَّلْمُّن ْس ِى ُّ لِلْا َّرْوَّإحِ ن ِالص ُّوَّرِ   عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ق َّا َّجَّلْث َّ عَّلَّى ع َّان ِث ٍ  
ق َّق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم لَّي ْهِمْ إ َّق ْزَّب 
ي َّسْمَّغُّ كَّلاَّمَّهُّمْ وَّ ي َّزَّى إ َّس ْح َّاصَّهُّمْ وَّ ي َّعْلَّمُّ إ َّسْزَّإرَّهُّمْ وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا إَّلْمَّ ح ْلُّوق ُّ إَّل َّذ ِى  إ ِذ َّإ إِن ْي َّق َّلَّ عَّن ْ مَّكَّان ٍ إِس ْي َّع َّلَّ ن ِهِ مَّكَّان   وَّ 
ج َّلاَّ مِ ن ْهُّ مَّكَّان   ق َّلاَّ ي َّذْرِى  ق ِى  إَّلْمَّكَّان ِ إَّل َّذ ِى  صَّارَّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ مَّا جَّذَّب َّ ق ِى  إَّلْمَّكَّان ِ إَّل َّذ ِى  كَّان َّ ق ِي هِ  ق َّا َّم َّا إَّلل َّهُّ إَّلْعَّط ِي مُّ إَّلس  َّا ْن ِ 
إَّلْمَّلِك ُّ إَّلذ َّي  َّان ُّ، ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ لأَّ ي َّح ْلُّو مِن ْهُّ مَّكَّان   وَّ لأَّ ي َّس ْي َّع ِلُّ ن ِهِ مَّكَّان   وَّ لأَّ ي َّكُّون ُّ إ ِلَّى مَّكَّان ٍ إ َّق ْ زَّب َّ مِن ْهُّ إ ِلَّى مَّكَّان ٍ وَّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ن َّعَّي َّهُّ 
ن ِالْأ ي َّاب ِ إَّلْمُّحْكَّمَّه ِ وَّ إَّلْن َّزَّإهِي  ن ِ إَّلْوَّإص ِحَّه ِ وَّ إ َّي  َّذَّةُّ ن ِن َّصْزِةِ وَّ إِج ْي َّارَّةُّ لِي َّن ْلِي غ ِ رِسَّالأَّي ِهِ صَّذ َّق ْن َّا ق َّوْلَّهُّ ن ِا َّن  َّ رَّن  َّهُّ ن َّعَّي َّهُّ   وَّ كَّل َّمَّه  
ق َّق َّامَّ عَّن ْهُّ إِن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْعَّوْج َّاءِ ق َّق َّالَّ لِأ َّصْحَّان ِهِ مَّن ْ إ َّلْق َّان ِى  ق ِى  ن َّحْزِ هَّذ َّإ سَّا َّلْي ُّكُّمْ إ َّن ْ ي َّلْي َّمِسُّ وإ لِى  ج ُّمْزَّة ً ق َّا َّلْق َّي ْي ُّمُّون ِى   
عَّلَّى ج َّمْزَّة ٍ  ق َّالُّوإ لَّهُّ مَّا كُّن ْث َّ ق ِى  مَّح ْلِسِهِ إ ِلأ َّ جَّق ِي زإً ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
In another narration:  
"The portion of the Kaaba allocated to Banu Hashim extended from the Black Stone to 
the Syrian Corner."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 60 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 216 
• Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 127 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 155 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[ 
WHOEVER INTENDS EVIL AGAINST THE KAABA 
No one has ever intended harm toward the Kaaba except that Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and 
Majestic, became angry on its behalf. Once, King Tubba’ resolved to kill the fighting 
men of the people of the Kaaba, take their offspring as captives, and destroy the 
Kaaba. His eyes began to swell until they fell onto his cheeks.  
He inquired about the cause, and they said: "We believe that what has afflicted you is 
due to your intention against this House, for the city is Allah’s {SWT} sanctuary, the 
House is Allah’s {SWT} House, and the residents of Mecca are the descendants of 
Ibrahim, the friend of Allah {SWT}."  
Tubba’ said: "You have spoken the truth. What can deliver me from what I am facing?"  
They said: "Change your intention and resolve to do good."  
So, he changed his intention to something better. His eyes returned to their proper 
place and were restored. He then summoned those who had advised him to destroy 
the Kaaba and killed them. Afterward, he went to the Kaaba, covered it with leather, 
and provided food for thirty days, sacrificing one hundred camels daily. Large trays of 
food were carried to the wild animals on the mountain peaks, and fodder was 
scattered for the beasts.  
He then departed from Mecca to Medina and settled there a group of Yemenites from 
Ghassan, who became the Ansar (supporters).   
It has been narrated:  
"Tubba’ sacrificed six thousand cattle at Sha'b Ibn 'Amir, a place that was referred to 
as the 'Kitchens of Tubba’' until it was later inhabited by Ibn 'Amir and became known 
as 'Sha'b Ibn 'Amir.' Tubba’ was neither a believer nor an outright disbeliever, but he 
sought the Abrahamic faith. None ruled over both the East and the West except Tubba’ 
and Khosrow.  
Later, the Kaaba was targeted by the companions of the elephant and their king, Abu 
Yaksum (Abraha ibn Al-Sabah Al-Himyari), who intended to demolish it. Allah {SWT} sent 
against them flocks of birds (Ababil) pelting them with stones of baked clay, rendering 
them like eaten straw.  
What happened to Tubba’ and the companions of the elephant did not befall Al-Hajjaj 
because his intention was not to destroy the Kaaba itself but to confront Ibn Al-Zubayr, 
who opposed the rightful authority. When Ibn Al -Zubayr sought refuge in the Kaaba, 
Allah {SWT} willed to show the people that He did not grant him safety, and so He delayed 
the punishment for its destruction upon Al-Hajjaj."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Monotheism, Part 1, p. 253 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 
403 • Al-Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 616 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 183 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2324

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2326  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق ِى  ج َّن َّزٍ إ ج َّزَّ : جَّذِي ث ٍ ي ُّذ ْكَّزُّ ق ِي هِ إَّلْأ ِسْلاَّمُّ وَّ إَّلْأ ِي مَّان ن  َّ رَّج ُّلاً ذَّج َّلَّ 
إَّلْكَّعْن َّه َّ ق َّن َّالَّ ق ِي هَّا مُّعَّان ِذإً إ ُّج ْزِح َّ مِن َّ إَّلْكَّعْن َّه ِ وَّ مِن َّ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ وَّ ص ُّزِن َّث ْ عُّن ُّق ُّه
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated from Isa ibn Yunus:   
Ibn Abi Al -Awja' was one of the students of Al -Hasan Al -Basri, but he deviated from 
monotheism. When asked why he abandoned his teacher's path and followed a belief 
with neither foundation nor truth, he said: "My teacher was inconsistent. At times, he 
spoke of free will (qadar), and at other times, of predestination (jabr). I do not know of 
him adhering to any belief consistently."   
He entered Mecca in rebellion and denial of those performing Hajj. Scholars disliked 
engaging with him in discussions or sitting with him because of his sharp tongue and 
corrupt intentions. However, he went to Imam Ja'far ibn Muhammad {a.s}, sat with him 
in a gathering of scholars, and said to him:   
"Discussions in such gatherings are a trust, and anyone with a cough must eventually 
cough. Will you permit me to speak?"  
Imam Ja'far {a.s} said: "Speak."  
Ibn Abi Al-Awja' then said: "For how long will you tread upon this threshing floor, cling 
to this stone, worship this house built of mud and clay, and run around it like a camel 
driven in frenzy? Whoever reflects on this or considers it carefully will realize that this 
is an act established by someone lacking wisdom and insight."   
Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s} replied: "Indeed, one whom Allah {SWT} has led astray and whose 
heart He has blinded finds truth bitter and fails to savor its sweetness. Satan becomes 
his ally, leading him to the waters of destruction without ever returning him from them.  
This is a House through which Allah {SWT} has subjected His creation to servitude, 
testing their obedience by their journey to it. He has urged them to venerate and visit 
it, making it the station of His Prophets and the direction (qiblah) for those who pray to 
Him. It is a branch of His pleasure and a pathway to His forgiveness, established in the 
utmost perfection and the gathering point of majesty and grandeur.   
Allah {SWT} created it two thousand years before the spreading of the earth. It is most 
fitting that He be obeyed in what He commands and refrained from what He {SWT} 
prohibits and warns against - Allah {SWT}, who forms souls and shapes them into their 
forms."  
Ibn Abi Al-Awja' responded: "You speak of something, O Abu Abdullah, and refer to the 
unseen."  
Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s} said: "Woe to you! How can He {SWT} be unseen, He who is 
present with His creation, nearer to them 'than their jugular vein,'  hears their speech, 
sees their forms, and knows their secrets? Only a created being becomes unseen 
when he moves from one place to another, so that the place he left becomes empty of 
him, and the place he reaches becomes occupied by him. Such a being cann ot know 
what occurs in the place he left while being in the place he arrived at."   
The Imam {a.s} continued: "As for Allah {SWT}, the Magnificent in status, the Sovereign, the 
Judge, no place is devoid of Him, no place preoccupies Him, and He is no closer to 
one place than to another. By the One who sent His Prophet with clear signs, definitive 
proofs, and supported him with His vi ctory, and who chose him to deliver His 
messages, we affirm his claim that his Lord {AZJ} sent him and spoke to him."  
Ibn Abi Al -Awja’ rose from the gathering and said to his companions:  "Who cast me 
into the ocean of this man's discourse? I asked you to find me a shade, and you threw 
me onto burning embers!"  
They replied: "You were insignificant in his assembly."   
He responded: "He is the son of the one who shaved the heads of those you see 
(referring to his lineage and the Imam's profound knowledge and authority)."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2325
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2327  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ْ ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝  وَّ مَّن ْ ذَّج َّلَّهُّ كٰان َّ إ مِن اً 
۝»وَّ مَّا ذَّج َّلَّ مِن َّ إَّلْوَّجْس ِ وَّ إَّلط َّي ْزِ كَّان 
إ مِن اً مِن ْ إ َّن ْ ي ُّهَّاح َّ إ َّوْ ي ُّو ْذ َّى جَّي  َّى ي َّح ْزُّح َّ مِن َّ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said in another narration, in a 
discussion about Islam and faith:  
"If a man were to enter the Kaaba and urinate in it out of defiance, he would be 
removed from the Kaaba and the Haram, and his neck would be struck."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 87 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 291 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2326
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
ومن  إ ي ى ن موج ث  إلحذ ق ى  إلحزم إج ذ  ن ه ق ى  إلحزم لأ ن ه لم ي ز للحزم جزمه .   
2328  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ : إ َّن  َّهُّ إ ُّي ِى َّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق ِي لَّ لَّهُّ إ ِن  َّ سَّن ُّعاً مِن ْ  سِن َّاعِ إَّلط َّي ْزِ عَّلَّى إَّلْكَّعْن َّه ِ  
لَّي ْسَّ ي َّمُّز ُّ ن ِهِ س َّى ْء  مِن ْ جَّمَّامِ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ إ ِلأ َّ ص َّزَّن َّهُّ ق َّق َّال هُّ وَّ إُّق ْي ُّلُّوةُّ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ ق َّذْ إ َّلْحَّذ
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about the words of Allah {SWT}, the 
Mighty and Majestic: "And whoever enters it shall be safe." (Surah Aal-e-Imran 3:97)  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Whoever enters the sanctuary (Haram) seeking refuge is safe from 
the wrath of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic. Likewise, any wild animal or bird that 
enters it is safe from being disturbed or harmed until it leaves the sanctuary."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 2, p. 95 • Tafsir al-Safi, Part 1, p. 
187 • Tafsir al-Safi, Part 1, p. 360 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 124 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, 
p. 370 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 145 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[ 
DEVIATION AND CRIMES IN THE SACRED SANCTUARY 
Whoever commits a crime warranting a prescribed punishment (hadd) within the 
Haram is to be punished within the Haram itself, for they have shown no regard for the 
sanctity of the Haram.

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2327

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2329  - ق َّالَّ : وَّ سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝ وَّ مَّن ْ ي ُّزِذْ ق ِي هِ ن ِا ِلْحٰاذٍ ن ِط ُّلْمٍ ن ُّذ ِق ْهُّ مِن ْ عَّذ ٰإب ٍ إ َّلِي مٍ ۝» 
ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated:   
It was brought to the attention of Abu Abdullah {a.s} that a predatory bird had taken up 
a position on the Kaaba and was attacking any of the Haram's pigeons that passed by.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Set up for it and kill it, for it has committed desecration (ilhad)."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 227 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 453 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 85 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 83 • Tafsir al-Burhan, vol. 3, p. 868 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 
96, p. 152 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 482 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Vol. 9, p. 71 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2328

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2330  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ إ َّن ِى  إَّلص َّن  َّاحِ إَّلْكِن َّان ِى ِّ عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ق ْسَّهُّ ن ِمَّك َّه َّ مِن ْ سَّزِق َّه ٍ 
إ َّوْ ط ُّلْمِ إ َّجَّذٍ إ َّوْ س َّى ْءٍ مِن َّ إَّلط  ُّلْمِ ق َّا ِن ِّى  إ َّرَّإةُّ إ ِلْحَّاذإً وَّ لِذ َّلِك َّ كَّان َّ ي َّي  َّ ق ِى  إَّلْق ُّق َّهَّاءُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّسْكُّن ُّوإ مَّك َّه
hadiths.en:
He (Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar) said:  
I asked Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s} about the words of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic:  
"And whoever intends a deviation therein or wrongdoing, We will make him taste a 
painful punishment." (Surah Al-Hajj 22:25)  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Every act of injustice is a form of deviation (ilhad), and striking a 
servant without cause is among such deviation."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 227 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 85 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2329
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٢٩

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
3312  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ : عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّزِي ذُّ مَّك َّه َّ إ َّوِ إَّلْمَّذِي ن َّه َّ إ َّ ي ُّكْزَّةُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّح ْزُّح َّ مِن ْهُّ ن ِالسِّلاَّحِ ق َّق َّال 
ي َّح ْزُّح َّ ن ِالسِّلاَّحِ مِن ْ ن َّلَّذِةِ وَّ لَّكِن ْ إ ِذ َّإ ذَّج َّلَّ مَّك َّه َّ لَّمْ ي ُّط ْهِزْةُّ.
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Abu Al-Sabbah Al-Kinani, from Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s}:  
"Every act of injustice a person commits against himself in Mecca - be it theft, 
oppression of someone, or any form of wrongdoing - I consider it to be deviation 
(ilhad). For this reason, the jurists avoided residing in Mecca."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2   
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[ 
DISPLAYING WEAPONS IN MECCA

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2330
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٣٠

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2332  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ جَّزِي ز ِ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال لاَّحٍ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّذْج ِلَّهُّ 
ق ِى  ج ُّوَّإلِق ٍ إ َّوْ ي ُّع َّي ِّن َّهُّ ي َّعْن ِى  جَّي  َّى ي َّلُّف  َّ عَّلَّى إَّلْحَّذِي ذِ س َّي ْن اً
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir asked Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man intending to travel to 
Mecca or Medina and whether it is disliked for him to carry weapons.   
Imam {a.s} replied: "There is no harm in carrying weapons when leaving his hometown, 
but when he enters Mecca, he should not display them."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2331
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٣١

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
3332  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلْمَّلِك ِ ن ْن ُّ عُّي ْن َّه َّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّم َّا ي َّصِلُّ إ ِلَّي ْن َّا مِن ْ ي ِي َّاب ِ إ لْكَّعْن َّه ِ هَّلْ ي َّصْلُّحُّ لَّن َّا إ َّن ْ 
ن َّلْن َّسَّ س َّي ْن اً مِن ْهَّا ق َّق َّال ن ْ س َّاءَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّعَّالَّى
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Hariz ibn Abdullah, from Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s}:  
"It is not appropriate to enter the Haram with a weapon unless it is placed in a sack or 
concealed - meaning, the blade should be wrapped in something."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 228 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 88 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 256 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[ 
BENEFITING FROM THE COVERING OF THE KAABA

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2332
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٣٢

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2334  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّعَّاوِي َّه َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّج َّذ ْب ُّ سُّك اً مِن ْ سُّك  إَّلْمَّق َّامِ وَّ ي ُّزَّإن اً 
مِن ْ ي ُّزَّإب ِ إَّلْن َّي ْث ِ وَّ سَّن ْغَّ جَّصَّي َّاب ٍ ق َّق َّال صَّى ق َّزُّذ َّة
hadiths.en:
Abdul Malik ibn Utbah asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about garments from the 
coverings of the Kaaba that reach us. He inquired whether it is permissible to wear any 
of it.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It is suitable for children, for covering copies of the Quran, and for 
use as cushions, seeking blessings through it, if Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, wills."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[ 
DISLIKE OF TAKING THE DUST AND PEBBLES OF THE KAABA

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2333
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٣٣

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2335   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  مِن ْ ي ُّزْن َّه ِ مَّا جَّوْلَّ  
إَّلْن َّي ْث ِ وَّ إ ِن ْ إ َّج َّذ َّ مِن ْ ذ َّلِك َّ س َّي ْن اً رَّذ َّةُّ.
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar: I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "I 
took some dust from the Maqam and soil from the House, along with seven pebbles."   
Imam {a.s} replied: "What you have done is not good. As for the dust and the pebbles, 
return them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 229 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 92 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 5, p. 232 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 220 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 3, p. 367 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2334
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٣٤

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2336  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ جُّذ َّي ْق َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّن ْصُّورٍ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إ ِن  َّ عَّمِّى  كَّن َّسَّ إَّلْكَّعْن َّه َّ ق َّا َّج َّذ َّ مِ ن ْ ي ُّزَّإن ِهَّا ق َّن َّحْن ُّ 
ن َّي َّذَّإوَّى ن ِهِ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Abdullah {a.s}:  
"It is not appropriate for anyone to take soil from around the House (Kaaba), and if 
someone takes anything from it, they must return it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 453 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2335
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٣٥

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar: 2337  - وَّ ق َّالَّ لَّهُّ ر َّي ْذ  إَّلس  َّح َّامُّ : إ ُّج ْزِح ُّ مِن َّ إَّلْمَّسْح ِذِ جَّصَّاة ً ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Hudhayfah ibn Mansur said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}:  
"My uncle swept the Kaaba and took some of its soil, and we use it for treatment."   
Imam {a.s} replied: "Return it to the Kaaba."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2336
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٣٦

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2338  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ن ْ ي ُّق ِي مَّ ن ِمَّك َّه َّ سَّن َّه ً 
ق ُّلْث ُّ كَّي ْف َّ ي َّصْن َّغُّ ق َّال لْكَّعْن َّه ِ
hadiths.en:
Zayd Al-Shahham said to Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s}:  
"May I take a pebble out of the mosque?"   
Imam {a.s} replied: "Return it or place it in another mosque."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[   
DISLIKE OF RESIDING IN MECCA

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2337
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٣٧

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated by Al-‘Ala’ from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja‘far 
{a.s}, who said: "It is not appropriate for a person to reside in Mecca for a full year."   
I asked: "What should he do?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He should move away from it. Also, it is not appropriate for any 
building to be raised higher than the Kaaba."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 230 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 446 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 448 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 463 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 88 • Wasa’il Al-
Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 233 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 81 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2338
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2340  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ذَّإوُّذُّ إَّلز َّق ِّى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ارْج ِغْ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ إ َّس ْوَّق ُّ لَّك َّ  
إ ِلَّى إَّلز ُّج ُّوعِ.
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated: "Residing in Mecca hardens the heart."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 230 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 89 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 234 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2339
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٤، ح ٢٣٣٩

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2341  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّعَّاوِي َّه َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ س َّح َّزَّة   إ َّصْلُّهَّا ق ِى  إ لْحِلِّ وَّ ق َّزْعُّهَّا ق ِى   
إَّلْحَّزَّمِ ق َّق َّال ق َّزْعَّهَّا ق ِى  إَّلْحِلِّ ق َّال 
إ َّصْلِهَّا
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated by Dawud Al-Raqqi from Abu Abdullah {a.s} that he said:  
"When you have completed your rituals, then return, for it will make you more eager to 
come back."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 230 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 89 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 234 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[   
TREES OF THE SACRED SANCTUARY

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2340
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.ar:
2342  -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّزِي ز   عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال اسِ إ َّج ْمَّعِي ن َّ إ ِلأ َّ 
مَّا إ َّن ْن َّي  َّهُّ إ َّن ْث َّ إ َّوْ ع َّزَّسْي َّهُّ.
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar:  I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} 
about a tree whose root is in the non -sacred area (hill) and its branches extend into 
the sacred area (Haram).  
Imam {a.s} replied: "The root is sanctified because of the branch's location."   
I then asked: "What if its root is in the Haram and its branch extends into the hill?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "The branch is sanctified because of the root's location."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 231 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 453 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 379 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 98 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 559 • Wasa’il Al-
Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 560 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 153 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2341

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON ORIGIN OF THE KAABA, ITS VIRTUE,
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated from Hariz, from Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s}, that he said:  
"Everything that grows in the Haram is forbidden for all people, except for what you 
yourself plant or cultivate."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 380 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 
12, p. 553 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 197 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 64, H. 2342
